mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and relaxes financial conditions, so month-over-month declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If 20-day SPX returns are not positive on average after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the sign flips and easing periods produce negative excess returns in a rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and rising recession risk, which depresses equity earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return after triggers is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand acceleration, supporting sales and revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive returns do not concentrate in periods when both RSAFS and HOUST trend upward together, or if either series alone explains the signal better than the joint interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO captures improving industrial capacity utilization and operating leverage, which should lift earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not stronger after positive INDPRO changes than after flat or negative changes, or if the rolling 1-year effect turns persistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A deep quarterly contraction in GDPC1 signals collapsing aggregate demand and lower expected corporate profits, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines on the latest quarterly observation
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 declines are followed by non-negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the sign of the effect reverses across two consecutive quarterly releases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound, real rates fall and liquidity conditions improve, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.50%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-and-policy trigger does not outperform the unconditional average, or if positive returns disappear once FEDFUNDS moves above the trigger bound.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE signals a genuine employment shock, which weakens earnings expectations and should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect is indistinguishable from either labor series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-lockdown reopening regime, rising HOUST combined with improving PAYEMS indicates reactivation of household demand and construction activity, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST-and-PAYEMS improvement fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns during reopening periods, or if the signal is absent outside the specified regime.
